fields.description:
# Code-review near-term nit reduction + new-behavior-without-test landmine detector

## Why
Code-review advisory volume is ~5 findings/review, dominated by docs + llm-prompts nits (0/9 high-value in
adjudication), while the highest-value landmine - new behavior shipped with no test - is buried by the old
scalar (0.415). We want less nit friction and a reliable catch for the landmine class.

## Scope cohesion (why these two changes ship as ONE child)
Both are small, config-adjacent changes to the SAME surface — code-review advisory NOISE — and the epic
(foliaged-merry-collie) scopes child 7 explicitly as "near-term nit reduction + new-behavior-without-test
detector". They are delivered in ONE reviewable commit but are INDEPENDENTLY tested (separate test files,
below) and INDEPENDENTLY revertible (the routing edit and the detector are separate hunks). Splitting into
two child tickets was considered and rejected: the ticket/gate overhead is disproportionate to two ~small,
same-surface, both-advisory-only changes; neither hard-blocks at rollout.

## What
- Down-rank/suppress the `docs` and `llm-prompts` advisory surfaces (and the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-
  landed-sibling' false-major pattern) in `code_review/criteria_routing.json`.
- Add a 'new-behavior-without-test' detector, shipped advisory/permissive first (the most objective landmine
  class).

## Detector mechanism (grounded in the ACTUAL codebase)
A purely textual regex cannot reliably identify 'new public behavior', so this is a GROUNDING-ASSISTED
heuristic (fail-open on ambiguity). It reuses rebar's REAL grounding surfaces (per docs/grounding.md the
available backends are the **ast-grep** fast lane and the **universal-ctags** T1 lane, plus an opt-in
confirm-only **pyright** backend — there is NO 'grep-ast' backend; that was a mis-statement):
- ADDED-DEFINITION SET: parse the unified diff into (file, added-lines); use the existing diff/reference
  machinery (`rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to
  identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS in the added lines (language-aware
  via the grounding backend; NOT a bare regex).
- TEST-CORRELATION: check whether the SAME change adds or modifies a test file (path matches the repo's test
  globs, e.g. `tests/`, `test_*.py`, `*_test.*`) that references any added symbol by name.
- FIRE: an added exported symbol with NO correlated test change -> ONE advisory 'new-behavior-without-test'
  finding. ABSTAIN (fail-open, NO finding, never raises, never blocks) when: the diff is unparseable/garbled;
  no grounding backend is available; or the change is docs/config-only (no code definitions added).
- ARCHITECTURE NOTE (honest): this is a PYTHON detector in `code_review/detectors.py`, invoked in the
  code-review DET phase (the `run_detectors` slice). It is MORE involved than the single-rule YAML smell
  detector (`grounding/detectors/builtin/smell_conflict_markers.yaml`) because it needs diff-parse +
  cross-file test correlation, not one ast-grep pattern — so it is registered as a code-review detector, not
  authored as a builtin YAML smell rule. Coaching surfaces through the existing `code_review_coach` path.

## Scope / files
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py` (the new `new_behavior_without_test` detector, reusing the
  grounding diff/symbol machinery).
- `tests/unit/` (two suites, below). No LLM-priority-threshold blocking here (that depends on the code-review
  impact redesign child).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs` + `llm-prompts` advisory surfaces are down-ranked/suppressed in `code_review/criteria_routing.json`
      (kept non-blocking AND de-prioritised / suppressed from the surfaced advisory set). A unit test asserts
      the packaged routing marks both criteria non-blocking and down-ranked vs the other advisory criteria.
      Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_nit_routing.py -v`.
- [ ] A grounding-assisted `new_behavior_without_test` detector exists in `code_review/detectors.py`, using
      the real ast-grep/ctags grounding machinery (via `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` + symbol lanes)
      to find added exported symbols and correlate test changes; advisory/permissive (never blocking); it
      ABSTAINS (returns no finding, never raises) on an unparseable/ambiguous diff, a docs/config-only diff,
      or when no grounding backend is available.
- [ ] Regression eval cases in `tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py` (all green): (a) MUST-FIRE on a
      diff adding an exported symbol with no covering test; (b) MUST-NOT-FIRE on a diff that adds/updates a
      test referencing the added symbol; (c) ABSTAINS on a docs-only diff; (d) ABSTAINS on an
      unparseable/garbled diff. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py -v`.
- [ ] A test asserts the detector's finding, when it fires, surfaces through the code-review advisory path as
      an advisory (never blocking) at this rollout.
